<!-- Generated by agents-reverse-engineer v1.2.8 -->

# src/quality

Detects documentation drift, code duplication, and phantom path references across the project by comparing TypeScript/JavaScript exports against .sum file documentation, identifying duplicate symbols across files, and validating AGENTS.md path references.

## Contents

**[index.ts](./index.ts)** — Barrel-exports quality analysis API: `extractExports`, `checkCodeVsDoc`, `checkCodeVsCode`, `checkPhantomPaths`, `buildInconsistencyReport`, `formatReportForCli`, `formatReportAsMarkdown` from subdirectories; re-exports `InconsistencySeverity`, `CodeDocInconsistency`, `CodeCodeInconsistency`, `PhantomPathInconsistency`, `Inconsistency`, `InconsistencyReport` from types.ts.

**[types.ts](./types.ts)** — Defines `InconsistencySeverity` literal union ('info' | 'warning' | 'error'), `CodeDocInconsistency` interface with `missingFromDoc`/`missingFromCode` arrays and optional `purposeMismatch`, `CodeCodeInconsistency` interface with `files` array and `pattern` field, `PhantomPathInconsistency` interface with `referencedPath`/`resolvedTo`/`context` in `details`, `Inconsistency` discriminated union via `type` field, `InconsistencyReport` with `metadata` (timestamp, projectRoot, filesChecked, durationMs) and `summary` counts.

## Subdirectories

**[inconsistency/](./inconsistency/)** — Implements code-vs-doc and code-vs-code checkers via regex-based export extraction and reporter formatters (CLI text with severity tags, GitHub-flavored Markdown tables).

**[phantom-paths/](./phantom-paths/)** — Validates AGENTS.md path references against filesystem, resolves paths relative to AGENTS.md directory and project root, tries `.ts` variants for `.js` imports, captures 120-char context lines.

## Quality Check Pipeline

`inconsistency/code-vs-doc.extractExports` → regex `/^[ \t]*export\s+(?:default\s+)?(?:function|class|const|let|var|type|interface|enum)\s+(\w+)/gm` → identifier list.

`inconsistency/code-vs-doc.checkCodeVsDoc` → compares source exports against .sum content substring matches → `CodeDocInconsistency[]` with `missingFromDoc`/`missingFromCode`.

`inconsistency/code-vs-code.checkCodeVsCode` → builds `Map<string, string[]>` of export names to file paths → `CodeCodeInconsistency[]` for duplicates with `pattern='duplicate-export'`, `severity='warning'`.

`phantom-paths/validator.checkPhantomPaths` → extracts paths via `PATH_PATTERNS` (markdown links, backtick paths, prose paths) → resolves from AGENTS.md dir and project root → `PhantomPathInconsistency[]` with `referencedPath`, `resolvedTo`, `context`.

`inconsistency/reporter.buildInconsistencyReport` → aggregates typed issues → `InconsistencyReport` with `summary` counts (total, codeVsDoc, codeVsCode, phantomPaths, errors, warnings, info).

`inconsistency/reporter.formatReportForCli` → renders `[ERROR]`/`[WARN]`/`[INFO]` prefixed text.

`inconsistency/reporter.formatReportAsMarkdown` → renders pipe-delimited table with `Severity | Type | Description | Location`.

## Type Surface

`InconsistencySeverity` — 'info' | 'warning' | 'error'.

`CodeDocInconsistency` — `type: 'code-vs-doc'`, `filePath`, `sumPath`, `description`, `details.missingFromDoc`, `details.missingFromCode`, optional `details.purposeMismatch`.

`CodeCodeInconsistency` — `type: 'code-vs-code'`, `files`, `pattern`, `description`.

`PhantomPathInconsistency` — `type: 'phantom-path'`, `agentsMdPath`, `details.referencedPath`, `details.resolvedTo`, `details.context`.

`Inconsistency` — union of above three types.

`InconsistencyReport` — `metadata` (timestamp, projectRoot, filesChecked, durationMs), `issues: Inconsistency[]`, `summary` (total, codeVsDoc, codeVsCode, phantomPaths, errors, warnings, info).

## Behavioral Contracts

**Export Extraction Pattern:** `/^[ \t]*export\s+(?:default\s+)?(?:function|class|const|let|var|type|interface|enum)\s+(\w+)/gm` (inconsistency/code-vs-doc.ts `extractExports`) — captures identifiers from export declarations, ignores re-exports and comments.

**Path Extraction Patterns:** (phantom-paths/validator.ts `PATH_PATTERNS`)
- Markdown links: `/\[(?:[^\]]*)\]\((\.[^)]+)\)/g`
- Backtick paths: `/`((?:src\/|\.\.?\/)[^`]+\.[a-z]{1,4})`/g`
- Prose paths: `/(?:from|in|by|via|see)\s+`?(src\/[\w\-./]+)`?/gi`

**Path Skip Patterns:** (phantom-paths/validator.ts `SKIP_PATTERNS`)
- `/node_modules/`
- `/.git/`
- `/^https?:\/\//`
- `/{{\}/` — template placeholders
- `/$\{/` — template placeholders
- `/\*/` — globs
- `/\{[^}]*,[^}]*\}/` — brace expansion

**CLI Severity Tags:** `[ERROR]`, `[WARN]`, `[INFO]` (inconsistency/reporter.ts `formatReportForCli`) — prefix format for terminal output.

**Markdown Table Format:** Pipe-delimited with columns `Severity | Type | Description | Location` (inconsistency/reporter.ts `formatReportAsMarkdown`) — escapes backticks in paths.

## File Relationships

`inconsistency/code-vs-doc.extractExports` is imported by `inconsistency/code-vs-code.checkCodeVsCode` for shared symbol extraction logic. Both checkers return typed inconsistencies that `inconsistency/reporter.buildInconsistencyReport` aggregates. All exports are barrel-re-exported via `index.ts`.
